mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, producing negative 5-day returns when INDPRO rises, or the 5-day predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, forcing markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0 and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or the joint effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal generates negative returns during periods of rapid payroll growth exceeding 300k per month.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below 13,000 billion chained 2017 dollars signals a contraction in aggregate demand, prompting immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations when GDPC1 drops below the 13,000 threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized supply-labor expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns when both series move in the predicted direction during late-cycle expansions, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.
